---
title: "Your Title Here"
format:
  docx:
    # reference-doc: ../your-template.docx
    number-sections: false
csl: apa.csl
bibliography: references.bib
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
# | label: setup
import sys

from pyprojroot import here

sys.path.insert(0, str(here("scripts")))

TABLE_DIR = here("output/tables")
FIGURE_DIR = here("output/figures")

import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown


def table_with_caption(df: pd.DataFrame, caption: str) -> str:
    """Render a markdown table with the caption as the last row."""
    md = df.to_markdown(index=False)
    n_cols = len(df.columns)
    caption_row = "| " + caption + " |" + " |" * (n_cols - 1)
    return md + "\n" + caption_row


def figure_with_caption(image_path: str, caption: str) -> str:
    """Render a figure as a simple 2-row, 1-column markdown table."""
    return f"| |\n|---|\n| ![]({image_path}) |\n| {caption} |"
```

# Introduction

\[Introduction text goes here.\]

# Theory

\[Theory text goes here.\]

# Methodology

\[Methodology text goes here.\]

<!-- Example: include a table from output/tables/ -->
<!-- ```{python}
df = pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / "tab-descriptive-stats.csv")
Markdown(table_with_caption(df, "Table 1. Descriptive statistics"))
``` -->

<!-- Example: include a figure from output/figures/ -->
<!-- ```{python}
Markdown(figure_with_caption(
    str(FIGURE_DIR / "fig-example.png"),
    "Figure 1. An example figure.",
))
``` -->

# Results

\[Results text goes here.\]

# Discussion

\[Discussion text goes here.\]

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
